era: 2013-present. Blues Rock. E=7 -- raw electric blues, twin sibling chemistry, punk velocity. Rainier Valley.
associated_musicians: The Black Keys (comparable format and tradition)

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST RAINIER VALLEY RETURN SINCE DEGREE 173. 76 degrees between appearances. Eva and Cedric Walker, twin siblings, Rainier Valley. Sub Pop. Blues rock with punk force. The neighborhood that borders the Central District (Hendrix, degrees 245-246) returns to the engine.

Twin sibling two-piece: guitar + drums, no bass. The musical communication shorthand of shared lives. White Stripes comparison but deeper blues foundation: Chicago electric tradition, Muddy Waters through to punk-blues synthesis. Eva Walker is the defining blues rock guitarist in current Seattle.

Sub Pop current artist: The Black Tones are an active, ongoing band on Sub Pop, not a legacy act. Cobain (2019) and My Brother's Blood Machine (2024). Sub Pop's ongoing role as home for Seattle blues rock beyond the grunge-era identity.

cross_references.relationship: Blues-rooted guitar, Central District. The tradition The Black Tones extend. Geographic and cultural predecessor 3-4 degrees prior.
Blues rock sequence continues. Central District roots, Hollywood Records, Hendrix lineage made explicit.
76 degrees between Rainier Valley appearances. The neighborhood re-enters the engine.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 vs E=4. Electric blues force vs. coastal storm resonance. Two kinds of physical sound: string under electric tension and air through needle-laden branches.

milestones: FIRST RAINIER VALLEY BAND SINCE DEGREE 173

discography.label: Sub Pop
discography.note: Debut full-length. Title references Kurt Cobain. Raw blues rock statement of intent.

discography.title: My Brother's Blood Machine
discography.label: Sub Pop
discography.note: Second full-length. Deeper vocabulary, greater dynamic range.
